content.text:
Perfect! I now have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile my findings:

## Post Interactions Exploration - Comprehensive Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the iskrib_v3 codebase, here's how post interactions (like, comment, bookmark, share) are implemented:

---

### 1. **API Calls for Post Interactions** (client/API/Api.js)

**Like:**
- `clickLike(token, body)` - POST to `/like`
- Body includes `{journalId, receiverId}`
- Requires authentication

**Comment:**
- `addComment(token, body)` - POST to `/addComment`
- Body includes `{comments, postId, receiverId}`
- Requires authentication

**Bookmark:**
- `addBookmark(token, journalId)` - POST to `/addBoorkmark` (note: typo in endpoint)
- Body is the journalId
- `getBookmarks(cursor, limit, userId, token)` - GET `/getBookmarks` with pagination
- Requires authentication

**Share:**
- No dedicated API endpoint
- ShareMenu component (client/src/components/ShareMenu/ShareMenu.jsx) handles:
  - Copy link (clipboard)
  - Share to X (Twitter)
  - Share to Facebook
  - Native share API

**Other interactions:**
- `createRepost(token, {sourceJournalId, caption})` - POST to `/repost` (reposts with optional caption)
- `createCanvasRemix(token, payload)` - POST to `/canvas/remix`

---

### 2. **Backend Routes** (server/routes/routes.js)

| Interaction | Route | Method | Auth | Handler |

## Post Card Components Analysis Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the iskrib_v3 React app, here's a comprehensive analysis of post cards and their interactions:

### 1. POST CARD RENDERING

**Main Component:** `PostCards.jsx` (C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\PostCards.jsx)

Post cards are rendered in a feed using Framer Motion animations with infinite scroll pagination. Key rendering features:

- **Post Types:** Supports text posts and canvas posts (visual/drawing posts)
- **Card Structure:** Each card displays:
  - Author avatar with optional verified/badge rings (legend, og badges)
  - Author name and verified badge
  - Post date (formatted)
  - Post title (truncated at 55 characters)
  - Content preview (truncated preview text)
  - Thumbnail image (if available)
  - Reading time estimate
  - Interaction counts (likes, comments, bookmarks)
  - Action buttons for interactions

- **Special Card Types:**
  - **Repost cards:** Show a "Repost header badge" with reposter name and original post preview
  - **Canvas cards:** Display canvas preview instead of text content, with "Remix this Canvas" button

**Additional Post Card Components:**
- `ProfilePostCards.jsx`: User's own posts in profile (grid and list views)
- `VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx`: Other users' posts in visited profiles

### 2. NAVIGATION & POST DETAIL VIEW

**Routing Architecture:**
```
- /home/post/:journalId → ContentView.jsx
- /home/post/:journalId/:slug → ContentView.jsx (with URL-friendly slug)
```

**Navigation Trigger Points:**
- Clicking post title or content area
- Clicking post image
- Clicking comment icon
- Suggestion search results

**How Navigation Works:**
The app uses React Router's `useNavigate()` with `location.state` to pass post data:

```javascript
// From PostCards.jsx (line 343)
navigate(`/home/post/${encodeURIComponent(journal.id)}${postSlug ? `/${postSlug}` : ''}`, {
    state: { /* post metadata */ }
});
```

### 3. LIKE, COMMENT, BOOKMARK & SHARE INTERACTIONS

**Like Functionality** (Lines 135-155, 264-270 in PostCards.jsx)
- **Icon:** Dynamic heart SVG (filled red when liked)
- **Handler:** `handleClickLike()` via `useLikeMutation` hook
- **API:** `/like` endpoint (POST) using `clickLike()` from Api.js
- **Payload:** `{journalId, receiverId, senderImageUrl, sendername, senderEmail}`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - calls `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Optimistic Update:** Cache updated immediately before server response via React Query

**Comments** (Line 703-706)
- **Icon:** Comment bubble SVG
- **Handler:** Opens `CommentSection` component (animated overlay)
- **API:** Separate comments system with `addComment()` and `getComments()`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Comment Component:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\comments\comments.jsx`

**Bookmarks** (Lines 170-185, 275-300)
- **Icon:** Bookmark SVG (filled green when bookmarked)
- **Handler:** `handleClickBookmark()` via `useBookMarkMutation` hook
- **API:** `/addBoorkmark` endpoint (POST) using `addBookmark()` [note: typo in endpoint name]
- **Payload:** `{journalId}`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - calls `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Debounced:** Uses `debounce()` helper to prevent duplicate requests (100ms)
- **Toast Message:** Shows "Post was added to your Bookmarks" or "Post was removed" with 2.5s timeout
- **Optimistic Update:** Cache updated via React Query with toggle logic

**Share/Repost** (Lines 186-193, 715-727)
- **Repost Icon:** Bidirectional arrow SVG
- **Handler:** Opens `RepostModal` component
- **Auth Check:** Yes - `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Disabled Condition:** Hidden if viewing own post (`journal.users.id !== userId`)
- **Modal Interaction:** Allows optional caption (max 280 chars) before creating repost
- **API:** `/repost` endpoint (POST) via `createRepost()`
- **Repost Toast:** Shows success message after repost creation

**Share Menu** (Detail View Only - ContentView.jsx)
- **Icon:** Share icon (network/share symbol)
- **Handler:** Opens `ShareMenu` component
- **URL Generation:** Uses `getShareUrl()` helper to format shareable link
- **Not a backend API:** Uses browser's native sharing or URL copying

### 4. AUTHENTICATION CHECKS

**Auth Context:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\Context\useAuth.js`
- Provides: `session`, `user`, `openAuthModal`, `loading`, `showAuthModal`, `closeAuthModal`

**Auth Check Pattern Used Across All Interactions:**
```javascript
if(!session) {
    return openAuthModal();
}
```

**Where Auth Checks Occur:**
1. **PostCards.jsx:**
   - Profile click (line 58-61)
   - View content (line 66-68)
   - Like action (line 267)
   - Bookmark action (line 276)
   - Repost action (line 719)

2. **ContentView.jsx:**
   - Like action (line 189-191)
   - Bookmark action (line 213-215)
   - Follow action (line 245-247)
   - Repost action (line 586)
   - Comment action (line 256-258)

**Session Data Structure:**
- `session.access_token`: Bearer token for API requests
- `user.userData[0].id`: Current logged-in user ID
- `user.userData[0].image_url`, `name`, `user_email`

### 5. POST DETAIL/READING VIEW

**Component:** `ContentView.jsx` (C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\ContentViewer\ContentView.jsx)

**Layout Features:**
- Back button (hidden/shown on scroll with animation)
- Post title as H1 heading
- Author metadata section with:
  - Avatar (clickable to visit profile)
  - Name and verified badge
  - Follow button (hidden if own post)
  - Post date
  - Reading time estimate
- Action bar with:
  - Like button (shows count)
  - Comment button (shows count)
  - Bookmark button (shows count)
  - Repost button (hidden if own post)
  - Share button
- Full post content (Lexical editor read-only for text, CanvasViewer for canvas)
- Comment section (animated overlay)

**Repost-Specific Detail View:**
Shows two-level structure:
1. Top section: Reposter's name, date, and optional caption
2. Embedded section: Original post author info, title, and preview
3. Actionable click on embedded content navigates to original post

**Canvas Post Detail View:**
- Full canvas rendering via `CanvasViewer` component
- "Remix this Canvas" button to create derivative
- Passes canvas doc to editor on remix

**Data Fetching:**
```javascript
const { data: fetchedJournalData } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['journalById', journalId, userId],
    queryFn: ({ queryKey }) => getJournalById(queryKey[1], queryKey[2]),
    enabled: shouldFetchPost,
    staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5
});
```

**Follow Feature (Detail Only):**
- Follow/Following button for author
- Uses `useFollowMutation` hook
- Debounced to prevent rapid clicks
- Updates follow status optimistically via React Query

### 6. MUTATION & CACHING STRATEGY

**React Query Pattern (useMutation.js):**

All mutations include:
1. **onMutate:** Optimistic update to cache before server response
2. **onError:** Rollback cache if mutation fails
3. **onSettled:** Invalidate related query keys to refetch

**Example - Like Mutation Flow:**
1. Immediately update local state and cache
2. Send POST request to `/like`
3. If error occurs, rollback using `previousData`
4. Invalidate `['journals']`, `['userJournals']`, `['visitedProfileJournals']` query keys

**Cache Keys Invalidated:**
- `journals` - Main feed
- `userJournals` - Profile posts
- `visitedProfileJournals` - Visited user's posts
- `journalById` - Single post detail view
- `followsData` - Follow status
- `getNotifications` - Notification updates

### 7. INTERACTION GUARD RAILS

**Duplicate Request Prevention:**
- Uses `useRef` with `Set` to track in-flight requests per journal ID
- Prevents double-clicking from creating multiple mutations
- Example: `inFlightBookmarkIdsRef`, `inFlightLikeIdsRef`

**State Normalization:**
- Helper functions normalize boolean/string/number values
- `normalizeInteractionFlag()` - Converts various truthy values
- `normalizeCount()` - Ensures valid non-negative numbers

**Debouncing:**
- Bookmark: 100ms debounce
- Follow: Debounced via `debounceClickFollow()`
- Custom `debounce()` helper function

### 8. API ENDPOINTS USED

| Operation | Endpoint | Method | Auth | Payload |
|-----------|----------|--------|------|---------|
| Get Feed Posts | `/journals` | GET | No | limit, cursor, userId |
| Get Single Post | `/journal/:id` | GET | No | journalId, userId |
| Like Post | `/like` | POST | Yes | journalId, receiverId, sender info |
| Add Bookmark | `/addBoorkmark` | POST | Yes | journalId |
| Add Comment | `/addComment` | POST | Yes | postId, text |
| Get Comments | `/getComments` | GET | No | postId, limit, cursor |
| Create Repost | `/repost` | POST | Yes | sourceJournalId, caption |
| Add Follow | `/addFollows` | POST | Yes | followingId, followerId |
| Get Follow Status | `/getFollowsData` | GET | No | userId, loggedInUserId |
| Add Views | `/addViews` | POST | Yes | journalId |

### 9. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Composition:**
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app for text editing
- Each repost/detail view has its own Lexical composer instance for read-only display

**Animation Library:** Framer Motion
- Card entrance: `opacity: 0, scale: 0.95` → `1, 1`
- Modal overlays: Spring physics animations
- Back button: Fade in/out on scroll

**State Management:**
- React Context for auth state
- React Query for server state and caching
- Local `useState` for UI state (modals, expandables)

**Error Handling:**
- Mutations catch errors and display toast messages
- Missing/unavailable posts show "Post is unavailable" message
- Network errors rollback optimistic updates

---

This comprehensive architecture ensures smooth interactions with built-in auth guards, optimistic updates for responsiveness, and proper cache invalidation to keep data fresh across all views.

Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed report of my findings:

## Post Card Components Analysis Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the iskrib_v3 React app, here's a comprehensive analysis of post cards and their interactions:

### 1. POST CARD RENDERING

**Main Component:** `PostCards.jsx` (C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\PostCards.jsx)

Post cards are rendered in a feed using Framer Motion animations with infinite scroll pagination. Key rendering features:

- **Post Types:** Supports text posts and canvas posts (visual/drawing posts)
- **Card Structure:** Each card displays:
  - Author avatar with optional verified/badge rings (legend, og badges)
  - Author name and verified badge
  - Post date (formatted)
  - Post title (truncated at 55 characters)
  - Content preview (truncated preview text)
  - Thumbnail image (if available)
  - Reading time estimate
  - Interaction counts (likes, comments, bookmarks)
  - Action buttons for interactions

- **Special Card Types:**
  - **Repost cards:** Show a "Repost header badge" with reposter name and original post preview
  - **Canvas cards:** Display canvas preview instead of text content, with "Remix this Canvas" button

**Additional Post Card Components:**
- `ProfilePostCards.jsx`: User's own posts in profile (grid and list views)
- `VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx`: Other users' posts in visited profiles

### 2. NAVIGATION & POST DETAIL VIEW

**Routing Architecture:**
```
- /home/post/:journalId → ContentView.jsx
- /home/post/:journalId/:slug → ContentView.jsx (with URL-friendly slug)
```

**Navigation Trigger Points:**
- Clicking post title or content area
- Clicking post image
- Clicking comment icon
- Suggestion search results

**How Navigation Works:**
The app uses React Router's `useNavigate()` with `location.state` to pass post data:

```javascript
// From PostCards.jsx (line 343)
navigate(`/home/post/${encodeURIComponent(journal.id)}${postSlug ? `/${postSlug}` : ''}`, {
    state: { /* post metadata */ }
});
```

### 3. LIKE, COMMENT, BOOKMARK & SHARE INTERACTIONS

**Like Functionality** (Lines 135-155, 264-270 in PostCards.jsx)
- **Icon:** Dynamic heart SVG (filled red when liked)
- **Handler:** `handleClickLike()` via `useLikeMutation` hook
- **API:** `/like` endpoint (POST) using `clickLike()` from Api.js
- **Payload:** `{journalId, receiverId, senderImageUrl, sendername, senderEmail}`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - calls `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Optimistic Update:** Cache updated immediately before server response via React Query

**Comments** (Line 703-706)
- **Icon:** Comment bubble SVG
- **Handler:** Opens `CommentSection` component (animated overlay)
- **API:** Separate comments system with `addComment()` and `getComments()`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Comment Component:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\comments\comments.jsx`

**Bookmarks** (Lines 170-185, 275-300)
- **Icon:** Bookmark SVG (filled green when bookmarked)
- **Handler:** `handleClickBookmark()` via `useBookMarkMutation` hook
- **API:** `/addBoorkmark` endpoint (POST) using `addBookmark()` [note: typo in endpoint name]
- **Payload:** `{journalId}`
- **Auth Check:** Yes - calls `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Debounced:** Uses `debounce()` helper to prevent duplicate requests (100ms)
- **Toast Message:** Shows "Post was added to your Bookmarks" or "Post was removed" with 2.5s timeout
- **Optimistic Update:** Cache updated via React Query with toggle logic

**Share/Repost** (Lines 186-193, 715-727)
- **Repost Icon:** Bidirectional arrow SVG
- **Handler:** Opens `RepostModal` component
- **Auth Check:** Yes - `openAuthModal()` if not authenticated
- **Disabled Condition:** Hidden if viewing own post (`journal.users.id !== userId`)
- **Modal Interaction:** Allows optional caption (max 280 chars) before creating repost
- **API:** `/repost` endpoint (POST) via `createRepost()`
- **Repost Toast:** Shows success message after repost creation

**Share Menu** (Detail View Only - ContentView.jsx)
- **Icon:** Share icon (network/share symbol)
- **Handler:** Opens `ShareMenu` component
- **URL Generation:** Uses `getShareUrl()` helper to format shareable link
- **Not a backend API:** Uses browser's native sharing or URL copying

### 4. AUTHENTICATION CHECKS

**Auth Context:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\Context\useAuth.js`
- Provides: `session`, `user`, `openAuthModal`, `loading`, `showAuthModal`, `closeAuthModal`

**Auth Check Pattern Used Across All Interactions:**
```javascript
if(!session) {
    return openAuthModal();
}
```

**Where Auth Checks Occur:**
1. **PostCards.jsx:**
   - Profile click (line 58-61)
   - View content (line 66-68)
   - Like action (line 267)
   - Bookmark action (line 276)
   - Repost action (line 719)

2. **ContentView.jsx:**
   - Like action (line 189-191)
   - Bookmark action (line 213-215)
   - Follow action (line 245-247)
   - Repost action (line 586)
   - Comment action (line 256-258)

**Session Data Structure:**
- `session.access_token`: Bearer token for API requests
- `user.userData[0].id`: Current logged-in user ID
- `user.userData[0].image_url`, `name`, `user_email`

### 5. POST DETAIL/READING VIEW

**Component:** `ContentView.jsx` (C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\ContentViewer\ContentView.jsx)

**Layout Features:**
- Back button (hidden/shown on scroll with animation)
- Post title as H1 heading
- Author metadata section with:
  - Avatar (clickable to visit profile)
  - Name and verified badge
  - Follow button (hidden if own post)
  - Post date
  - Reading time estimate
- Action bar with:
  - Like button (shows count)
  - Comment button (shows count)
  - Bookmark button (shows count)
  - Repost button (hidden if own post)
  - Share button
- Full post content (Lexical editor read-only for text, CanvasViewer for canvas)
- Comment section (animated overlay)

**Repost-Specific Detail View:**
Shows two-level structure:
1. Top section: Reposter's name, date, and optional caption
2. Embedded section: Original post author info, title, and preview
3. Actionable click on embedded content navigates to original post

**Canvas Post Detail View:**
- Full canvas rendering via `CanvasViewer` component
- "Remix this Canvas" button to create derivative
- Passes canvas doc to editor on remix

**Data Fetching:**
```javascript
const { data: fetchedJournalData } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['journalById', journalId, userId],
    queryFn: ({ queryKey }) => getJournalById(queryKey[1], queryKey[2]),
    enabled: shouldFetchPost,
    staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5
});
```

**Follow Feature (Detail Only):**
- Follow/Following button for author
- Uses `useFollowMutation` hook
- Debounced to prevent rapid clicks
- Updates follow status optimistically via React Query

### 6. MUTATION & CACHING STRATEGY

**React Query Pattern (useMutation.js):**

All mutations include:
1. **onMutate:** Optimistic update to cache before server response
2. **onError:** Rollback cache if mutation fails
3. **onSettled:** Invalidate related query keys to refetch

**Example - Like Mutation Flow:**
1. Immediately update local state and cache
2. Send POST request to `/like`
3. If error occurs, rollback using `previousData`
4. Invalidate `['journals']`, `['userJournals']`, `['visitedProfileJournals']` query keys

**Cache Keys Invalidated:**
- `journals` - Main feed
- `userJournals` - Profile posts
- `visitedProfileJournals` - Visited user's posts
- `journalById` - Single post detail view
- `followsData` - Follow status
- `getNotifications` - Notification updates

### 7. INTERACTION GUARD RAILS

**Duplicate Request Prevention:**
- Uses `useRef` with `Set` to track in-flight requests per journal ID
- Prevents double-clicking from creating multiple mutations
- Example: `inFlightBookmarkIdsRef`, `inFlightLikeIdsRef`

**State Normalization:**
- Helper functions normalize boolean/string/number values
- `normalizeInteractionFlag()` - Converts various truthy values
- `normalizeCount()` - Ensures valid non-negative numbers

**Debouncing:**
- Bookmark: 100ms debounce
- Follow: Debounced via `debounceClickFollow()`
- Custom `debounce()` helper function

### 8. API ENDPOINTS USED

| Operation | Endpoint | Method | Auth | Payload |
|-----------|----------|--------|------|---------|
| Get Feed Posts | `/journals` | GET | No | limit, cursor, userId |
| Get Single Post | `/journal/:id` | GET | No | journalId, userId |
| Like Post | `/like` | POST | Yes | journalId, receiverId, sender info |
| Add Bookmark | `/addBoorkmark` | POST | Yes | journalId |
| Add Comment | `/addComment` | POST | Yes | postId, text |
| Get Comments | `/getComments` | GET | No | postId, limit, cursor |
| Create Repost | `/repost` | POST | Yes | sourceJournalId, caption |
| Add Follow | `/addFollows` | POST | Yes | followingId, followerId |
| Get Follow Status | `/getFollowsData` | GET | No | userId, loggedInUserId |
| Add Views | `/addViews` | POST | Yes | journalId |

### 9. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Composition:**
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app for text editing
- Each repost/detail view has its own Lexical composer instance for read-only display

**Animation Library:** Framer Motion
- Card entrance: `opacity: 0, scale: 0.95` → `1, 1`
- Modal overlays: Spring physics animations
- Back button: Fade in/out on scroll

**State Management:**
- React Context for auth state
- React Query for server state and caching
- Local `useState` for UI state (modals, expandables)

**Error Handling:**
- Mutations catch errors and display toast messages
- Missing/unavailable posts show "Post is unavailable" message
- Network errors rollback optimistic updates

---

This comprehensive architecture ensures smooth interactions with built-in auth guards, optimistic updates for responsiveness, and proper cache invalidation to keep data fresh across all views.

Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me summarize the needed changes:

### Current behavior:
1. **PostCards.jsx line 66-68**: `viewContent()` has `if(!session) return openAuthModal()` - blocks non-logged-in users from clicking post cards
2. **PostCards.jsx line 58-60**: `handleClickUserProfile` has `if(!session) return openAuthModal()` - blocks profile clicking
3. **ContentView.jsx**: Like (line 189-191), Bookmark (line 213-215), Repost (line 586) all have auth checks - these are GOOD, keep them


4. **ContentView.jsx line 256-258**: `handleClickComments` opens the comment section without checking auth, but submitting a comment requires a session token
5. **Comments component**: Shows the input area to everyone, but the backend rejects submissions from unauthenticated users with a 401 error

### Changes needed:

**1. PostCards.jsx - `viewContent` function (line 64-75)**
- Remove the auth check so guests can view content, but keep the views mutation wrapped to only fire for logged-in users

**2. PostCards.jsx - `handleClickUserProfile` function (line 56-62)**
- Remove the auth check so guests can click to view profiles

**3. ContentView.jsx - `handleClickComments` function (line 256-258)**
- Add an auth check that opens the modal if the user isn't logged in, preventing them from accessing the comment section

**4. Comments component**
- Add an auth check before rendering the comment input area so guests don't see it, but still show existing comments to everyone

For the profile navigation, I'm checking what happens when a guest clicks a profile link in ContentView. The `handleClickProfile` function doesn't have auth checks itself, but it relies on `user?.userData[0].id` which would be undefined for non-logged-in users. The function checks if the clicked user ID matches the logged-in user ID to decide whether to navigate to `/profile` or `/user-profile`.

However, there's a bug here—`user?.userData[0]` will throw an error if `user` is null because optional chaining stops at the first undefined but doesn't protect the bracket notation. I need to fix this to use `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` instead to safely handle the null case.

Looking at the rest of ContentView.jsx, I'm seeing the same pattern repeated across multiple lines. Lines 526, 532, and 534 all have similar issues where optional chaining isn't applied consistently to the array access and property lookups. Some lines like 558 and 581 already have the correct optional chaining syntax, but others need to be updated to match that pattern. Continuing through the rest of the file, I see line 532 has `user?.userData?.[0].id` in a comparison with `postData?.userId` - but that's still missing the final optional chaining on `.id`, so it would throw if userData or the array element is undefined. Lines 534 and 558 look fine with proper optional chaining throughout.

Let me trace through what happens with the auth context - there's a query being set up that fetches userData based on the authenticated user's ID, so I need to understand what `user` actually contains when someone's logged in versus when they're a guest. I see the issue now—when the user isn't logged in, `user?.userData?.[0]` evaluates to undefined, and then accessing `.image_url` on that undefined value crashes immediately when the click handler is invoked, before the auth check inside `handleClickLike` even gets a chance to run. The optional chaining needs to extend all the way through to each property being accessed.

Looking at the other crash points, `handleClickProfile` has the same problem on lines 518 and 526 where it's trying to access `user?.userData[0].id` without the optional chaining on the array access itself, and the follow button condition on line 532 will also fail when comparing undefined values.

So I need to tackle this in two ways: first, allow guests to view content by removing the auth check from `viewContent` and wrapping the mutation in a session check, plus adding an auth modal trigger to `handleClickComments`; second, fix all the unsafe property accesses throughout ContentView by properly chaining through the optional userData array access and its properties. _url` etc → add `?.` on lines 469, 558

**C) ContentView comment behavior for guests:**
6. The `hanldeClickComments` function (line 256-258) doesn't check auth - guests can open the comment overlay and see existing comments (reading comments is fine since `getComments` doesn't require auth), but the submit will silently fail (401). We should add an auth check to the comment submit, or better yet, hide the input area for non-logged-in users.

Actually, re-reading the user's request: "when if they interact: like, comment or bookmark except share they will need to log in and show the authModal()". So:
- Comment button should show authModal for guests
- Like button already has auth check ✓
- Bookmark button already has auth check ✓
- Share should work without login (it already does in ContentView - it's a client-side ShareMenu)

For the comment interaction, the user wants guests to see the auth modal when they try to comment, not open the overlay and fail silently. I need to add an auth check to the comment button click handler so it triggers the modal instead of opening the comment section. # Allow Guest Users to Read Posts

## Context
Currently, non-logged-in (guest) users can see the feed but clicking any post card shows the auth modal instead of navigating to the content. The goal is to let guests click post cards and read the full content, while still requiring login for interactions (like, comment, bookmark). Share should remain accessible without login.

## Changes

### 1. PostCards.jsx — Remove auth gate from `viewContent` (line 64-75)
**File:** `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx`

Remove the `if(!session) return openAuthModal()` check (lines 66-68). Wrap the `mutateViews.mutate()` call in `if(session)` so views are only tracked for logged-in users. The navigation via `clickContent()` will proceed for everyone.

```js
const viewContent = (e, ...) => {
    e.stopPropagation();
    // REMOVED: if(!session) return openAuthModal();
    if(session) {
        const formadata = new FormData();
        formadata.append('journalId', journalId);
        mutateViews.mutate(formadata);
    }
    clickContent(e, ...)
}
```

### 2. ContentView.jsx — Add auth check to comment button (line 256-258)
**File:** `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx`

Add `if(!session) return openAuthModal()` to `hanldeClickComments` so guests see the auth modal instead of opening the comment overlay.

### 3. ContentView.jsx — Add auth check to follow button (line 245-248)
Add `if(!session) return openAuthModal()` inside `handleClickFollow` so guests are prompted to log in.

### 4. ContentView.jsx — Fix null-safety crashes for guest users
When `user` is undefined (guest), several property accesses will throw TypeError. Fix all unsafe accesses:

| Line(s) | Current | Fix |
|---------|---------|-----|
| 469 | `user?.userData?.[0].image_url`, `.name`, `.user_email` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.image_url`, `?.name`, `?.user_email` |
| 518 | `user?.userData[0].id` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` |
| 526 | `user?.userData[0].id` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` |
| 532 | `user?.userData?.[0].id` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` |
| 534 | `user?.userData?.[0].id` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.id` |
| 558 | `user?.userData?.[0].image_url`, `.name`, `.user_email` | `user?.userData?.[0]?.image_url`, `?.name`, `?.user_email` |

## Files to Modify
1. `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` — 1 change (remove auth gate from viewContent)
2. `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — ~8 changes (auth checks + null safety)

## What Stays the Same
- Like button: already has auth check in both PostCards and ContentView
- Bookmark button: already has auth check in both PostCards and ContentView
- Repost button: already has auth check in both PostCards and ContentView
- Share button: already works without auth (client-side only)
- Profile click on PostCards feed: stays auth-gated (user didn't request changing this)
- Backend routes: all interaction endpoints already have `requireAuth` middleware

## Verification
1. Open app without logging in
2. Confirm post cards are visible on the feed
3. Click a post card — should navigate to ContentView and show full post content
4. Click like/bookmark/comment buttons — should show auth modal
5. Click share button — should work without auth modal
6. Confirm no JS crashes in console when viewing as guest
